title: [lichen_l2.c:955] lichen_l2_enable checks iface_init_failed but not link_ctx_initialized
description:
Contract: lichen_l2_enable should reject operations on uninitialized module.
Violation: The enable path (line 986 onwards) checks atomic_get(&link_ctx_initialized) and can re-initialize if false. However, it does this AFTER acquiring mutexes. If iface_init completely failed (never set link_ctx_initialized), the enable path would attempt re-init. This is intentional (documented at line 988-999), but relies on lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returning non-NULL.
Impact: If lora_l2 init succeeded but lichen_l2 iface_init failed partway, enable() could attempt recovery. This is arguably a feature, but the control flow is complex and could have edge cases.
Mitigation: Consider explicit state tracking for 'iface_init completed successfully' vs 'iface_init started but failed'. Current iface_init_failed atomic covers the failure case adequately.
close_reason: Current checks are sufficient. Unlike TX/RX paths (which must reject when link_ctx_initialized is false), lichen_l2_enable(state=true) is DESIGNED to handle uninitialized link_ctx by re-initializing it (lines 1022-1064). This handles enable after prior disable. The iface_init_failed check guards fundamental failures (missing LoRa device), and lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit() guards mutex deadlock after thread abort. Adding a link_ctx_initialized check would incorrectly reject legitimate re-enable operations.
